title: Kerala Plus Two Result 2026 declared: HSE pass percentage rises to 77.97%, 30,561 secure full A+
content: Thiruvananthapuram: The Kerala Higher Secondary Examination (HSE), Vocational Higher Secondary Examination (VHSE) and Technical Higher Secondary Examination (THSE) results for 2026 were announced at 3 pm on Tuesday. General Education and Minority Welfare Minister N Samsudheen officially declared the results at the PRD Press Chamber in the Secretariat. The overall pass percentage increased marginally by 0.16 percentage points to 77.97%, up from 77.81% last year. While boys recorded a pass percentage of 68.41%, girls outperformed them with 86.69%. The pass percentage in government schools stood at 72.66%, while aided schools recorded 82.82%. Nine government schools secured a 100% pass rate. A total of 2,90,398 students qualified for higher studies, while 30,561 students secured A+ in all subjects. Students can access their Plus Two results from 3.30 pm onwards through official websites, mobile applications and WhatsApp services provided by the Kerala government. Around 4.52 lakh students are awaiting the Higher Secondary examination results, while 26,826 students are waiting for the Vocational Higher Secondary results. Last year, the pass percentage was 77.81% in Higher Secondary and 70.06% in VHSE. Click here to check results.
